comments: Approved. The kickoff packet explicitly imports all V0.7 carry-overs into V0.8 scope: .agent-state/ physical migration, SubAgent review automation daemon, and legacy tooling deprecation (tasks/v0.8-kickoff-packet.md:12-19), with matching implementation scope in tasks/v0.8-scope-definition.md:5-10 and tasks/v0.8-requirements-analysis.md:19-22. The new core requirements are explicit: Dynamic Participant Registry and capability-based routing via dual-tier topology are defined in tasks/v0.8-kickoff-packet.md:17-20, tasks/v0.8-planning-q-and-a.md:6-10 and 34-41, and tasks/v0.8-requirements-analysis.md:6-18. The kickoff materials answer the orchestration, registry, and workspace-topology questions directly, including decentralized pull-based team claiming and local .agent-state/ execution in the personal domain. Executor bypass is explicitly forbidden: tasks/v0.8-scope-definition.md:14 states the Executor must not generate its own decision.json, and tasks/v0.8-milestone-map.md:15-19 keeps isolated Gatekeeper invocation in scope while making self-approval out of scope. The required topology is explicit: personal-domain execution uses the local .agent-state/ workspace, while remote teams fall back to a dedicated cloud sync branch such as agent-sync for milestone/gate-level synchronization (tasks/v0.8-planning-q-and-a.md:25-41; tasks/v0.8-requirements-analysis.md:14-17; tasks/v0.8-milestone-map.md:21-24). Criterion 6 also passes for this kickoff planning phase: git status shows only new planning artifacts under tasks/v0.8-*.md/json, with no src/ implementation edits and no workflow/dispatch.json semantic changes present in the workspace.
